visibilitySummary: Annular path crosses Antarctica; partial visibility from southern Argentina, southern Chile, and southern Africa.
notes: Saros 121. Source: NASA eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov solar eclipse decade table, fetched 2026-07-18.

visibilitySummary: Totality crosses the Arctic, Greenland, Iceland, and northern Spain; partial visibility across most of Europe, northern Africa, and eastern North America.
notes: Saros 126. Source: NASA eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov solar eclipse decade table, fetched 2026-07-18.

visibilitySummary: Annular path over the South Atlantic and southern Chile/Argentina; partial visibility from South America, southern Africa, and Antarctica.
notes: Saros 131. Source: NASA eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov solar eclipse decade table, fetched 2026-07-18.

visibilitySummary: Totality crosses Morocco, Spain, Algeria, Libya, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, Yemen, and Somalia; partial visibility across Africa, Europe, the Middle East, and western/southern Asia.
notes: Saros 136. Maximum totality ~6m23s near Luxor, Egypt -- the longest total solar eclipse over easily accessible land in the 21st century (next longer: 2114-06-03). Sources: NASA eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov; Space.com 'Total solar eclipse 2027: A complete guide'; Sky & Telescope 'Luxor 2027: A Total Solar Eclipse for the Ages' -- all fetched 2026-07-18.

visibilitySummary: Annular path crosses Ecuador, Peru, Brazil, Suriname, Spain, and Portugal; partial visibility across eastern North America, Central/South America, and western Europe.
notes: Saros 141. Source: NASA eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov solar eclipse decade table, fetched 2026-07-18.

visibilitySummary: Totality crosses Australia and passes near New Zealand; partial visibility across Southeast Asia and the East Indies.
notes: Saros 146. Source: NASA eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov solar eclipse decade table, fetched 2026-07-18.

visibilitySummary: Partial eclipse visible across North America and Central America.
notes: Saros 151. No central (total/annular) path -- partial everywhere it is visible. Source: NASA eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov solar eclipse decade table, fetched 2026-07-18.

visibilitySummary: Partial eclipse visible from the Arctic, Scandinavia, Alaska, northern Canada, and northern Asia.
notes: Saros 118. No central (total/annular) path. Source: NASA eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov solar eclipse decade table, fetched 2026-07-18.

visibilitySummary: Partial eclipse visible from southern Chile and southern Argentina.
notes: Saros 156. No central (total/annular) path. Source: NASA eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov solar eclipse decade table, fetched 2026-07-18.

visibilitySummary: Partial eclipse visible from southern Argentina, southern Chile, and Antarctica.
notes: Saros 123. No central (total/annular) path. Source: NASA eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov solar eclipse decade table, fetched 2026-07-18.

visibilitySummary: Annular path crosses Algeria, Tunisia, Greece, Turkey, Russia, northern China, and Japan; partial visibility across Europe, North Africa, the Middle East, and most of Asia.

visibilitySummary: Totality crosses Botswana, South Africa, and Australia; partial visibility across southern Africa, the southern Indian Ocean, and the East Indies.
notes: Saros 133. Source: NASA eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov solar eclipse decade table, fetched 2026-07-18.

visibilitySummary: Annular path crosses Angola, the Congo, Zambia, Tanzania, southern India, Malaysia, and Indonesia.
notes: Saros 138. Times listed by NASA as Terrestrial Dynamical Time (TD), within about a minute of UTC at this epoch -- immaterial at this table's coarse precision. Source: NASA eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov, fetched 2026-07-18.

visibilitySummary: Hybrid eclipse over the Pacific and Panama; partial visibility across the southern United States, Central America, and northwestern South America.
notes: Saros 143. Times listed by NASA as Terrestrial Dynamical Time (TD), within about a minute of UTC at this epoch. Source: NASA eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov, fetched 2026-07-18.

visibilitySummary: Broadly visible from the Pacific, the Americas, and eastern Asia/Australia per NASA's global visibility map; exact local visibility depends on whether the Moon is above the horizon at peak -- computed live from the observer's location.
notes: Source: NASA eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov lunar eclipse decade table, fetched 2026-07-18.

visibilitySummary: Broadly visible from the Americas, Europe, Africa, and the Atlantic/Pacific per NASA's global visibility map; exact local visibility depends on whether the Moon is above the horizon at peak -- computed live from the observer's location.
notes: Deep partial eclipse (over 90% of the Moon's disc in Earth's umbra). Source: NASA eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov lunar eclipse decade table; Space.com 'August 2026 lunar eclipse' -- fetched 2026-07-18.

visibilitySummary: Penumbral eclipses are subtle -- only a faint shading on the Moon's disc, not the dramatic darkening of a total or partial eclipse. Local visibility depends on whether the Moon is above the horizon at peak -- computed live from the observer's location.
notes: Source: NASA eclipse.gsfc.nasa.gov lunar eclipse decade table, fetched 2026-07-18.
